title: Alzheimer's disease drug development pipeline: 2026
authors: J. Cummings; Yadi Zhou; Yuxin Yang; Kate Zhong; Jorge Fonseca; A. L. Osse; Feixiong Cheng
journal: Alzheimer's & Dementia : Translational Research & Clinical Interventions
abstract: Abstract INTRODUCTION Discovery and development of new therapies for Alzheimer's disease (AD) are urgently needed to address the world's growing population of individuals on the AD pathophysiological continuum. Clinicaltrials.gov is a resource for studying drugs in development for treatment of AD. RESULTS There are currently 158 drugs in 192 AD trials. Of the agents in trials, 39% are small molecule disease targeting therapies (DTTs); 34% are biologic DTTs; 18% are cognition enhancing symptom targeted therapies (STTs); and 10% are STTs being developed to treat neuropsychiatric symptoms of AD. Currently active trials require 54,728 participants of which 38,417 are in Phase 3. The biopharmaceutical industry sponsors 59% of AD trials including 72% of Phase 3 trials. Repurposed drugs represent 35% of the drugs in trials. DISCUSSION The AD drug development pipeline has a growing number of trials and drugs in trials. A diverse array of AD pathophysiological processes is being addressed by drugs in trials.
